top_categories.workout_context: 8,800ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At mile 60 of 81, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
109ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At The Majestics, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Race-pace efforts that mimic The Majestics' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 81 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
The Majestics' mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 81 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of The Majestics. Train it until it feels boring.
8,800ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Low-cadence force work for 8,800ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At The Majestics, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 81 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.heat: July in Villars-sur-Ollon, Vaud, Switzerland can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before The Majestics. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for The Majestics’s 81 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: The Majestics with 8,800ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at The Majestics requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Durability, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 81 miles of mixed terrain in Villars-sur-Ollon, Vaud, Switzerland.
